extraction.one_line_summary: ACKERMANN Pascal won a technical wet sprint in Qinzhou, defeating JAKOBSEN Fabio in a textbook BORA-hansgrohe leadout on flooded roads.
tactical_patterns: BORA-hansgrohe delivered textbook leadout in treacherous wet conditions while other sprint trains faltered in flooding, exposing positioning discipline gap.
GRØNEWEGEN Dylan lost despite holding race lead, suggesting points jersey pressure diverted team resources from optimal sprint train organization.
Five-rider break with three TT specialists (DOWSETT, GRIVKO, DOUCET) still controlled easily, revealing underpowered breakaway selection in short flat stages.

rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: positive: eighth win of season shows consistent sprint form, fourth World Tour victory demonstrates elevation to top-tier sprinter status.
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: negative: DNS stage two, career-ending retirement from BMC in poor weather conditions suggests injury or illness accelerated decision.
rider_signals.GRIVKO Andrey: positive: second consecutive day in breakaway despite mechanical indicates exceptional recovery and opportunistic breakaway positioning instincts.
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: vanquished in sprint despite red jersey and points lead suggests team tactical focus compromised by jersey defense duties.
rider_signals.DOWSETT Alex: positive: aggressive in break on flat stage atypical for pure time trialist, signals improved road racing positioning beyond TT specialty.
rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: positive: second place against strong field in difficult conditions confirms consistent sprint threat even without optimal leadout.
rider_signals.NAVARDAUSKAS Ramūnas: negative: vehicle collision forced retirement, reveals increased danger of team car proximity in wet peloton conditions.
